person.name: Joan de Braose

memoryArtifacts.headline: Arms attributed to William de Braose by Matthew Paris

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Biography
Joan de Brewes was born to William de Brewes and his first wife, Agnes. She married James de Bohun, son of Sir John de Bohun and Joan de la Chapelle, circa 1301. They had 1 son:

1.) John, Knt. b c 1301

Her first husband, James de Bohun, died before 30 may 1306.

Joan de Brewes and Sir Richard Foliot, 2nd Lord Filiot obtained a marriage license on 16 September 1310. They had three children:

1.) Richard

2.) Margery, married Hugh de Hastings

3.) Margaret, married John de Camoys

Joan de Brewes died before 23 June 1324 at of Gressenhall & Weasenham, Norfolk, England.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Braose於大約1283年出生在英國素可布拉波 卡素時，她的父親William de Braose 2nd Baron Braose24歲，母親Agnes de Sully21歲。她於1301年與James de Bohun在英國素可布拉波結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1324年6月23日在英國英格蘭諾福爾可格雷哈爾享年42歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Braose于大约1283年出生在英国素可布拉波 卡素时，她的父亲William de Braose 2nd Baron Braose24岁，母亲Agnes de Sully21岁。她于1301年与James de Bohun在英国素可布拉波结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1324年6月23日在英国英格兰諾福爾可格雷哈爾享年42岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Braose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Eckhard, Gerhard, Manfred, Otto.
from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Ambrosius (see Ambrose ).

variants.name: Ambrose
meanings: English and Scottish: from the personal name Ambrose (French Ambroise, Latin Ambrosius, from Greek ambrosios ‘immortal, divine’), which was popular throughout Christendom in medieval Europe. Its popularity was due in part to the fame of Saint Ambrose (c. 340–397), one of the four Latin Fathers of the Church, the teacher of Saint Augustine. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Czech, Slovenian, and Croatian Ambrož (see Ambroz ), and also their derivatives, e.g. the Slovenian patronymic Ambrožič.
Irish: from Mac Ambróis ‘son of Ambrose’ (see 1 above); a West Munster name, which has also been Anglicized as McCambridge .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Braose was born about 1283, in Bramber Castle, Sussex, England, her father, William de Braose 2nd Baron Braose, was 24 and her mother, Agnes de Sully, was 21. She married James de Bohun in 1301, in Bramber, Sussex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 23 June 1324, in Gressenhall, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 42.

fr.Gower, Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom: Gower, Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Braose est née environ 1283, à Bramber Castle, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, William de Braose 2nd Baron Braose, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Agnes de Sully, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé James de Bohun en 1301, à Bramber, Sussex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 23 juin 1324, à Gressenhall, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 42 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Braose um 1283 in Bramber Castle, Sussex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Braose 2nd Baron Braose 24 und ihre Mutter Agnes de Sully 21. Sie hat 1301 in Bramber, Sussex, England James de Bohun geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 23. Juni 1324 im Alter von 42 Jahren in Gressenhall, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

Lei sposato James de Bohun nel 1301, nel Bramber, Sussex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 23 giugno 1324, nel Gressenhall, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 42 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Braose(이)가 대략 1283년 영국 수세스 브램버 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Braose 2nd Baron Braose(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes de Sully(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1301년 영국 수세스 브램버에서 James de Bohun(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1324년 6월 23일 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 그레센홀에서 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Braose nasceu aproximadamente 1283, em Bramber Castle, Sussex, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de Braose 2nd Baron Braose, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Agnes de Sully, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com James de Bohun em 1301, em Bramber, Sussex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 23 de junho de 1324, em Gressenhall, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 42 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Braose родилась около 1283 г. в местности брэмбир кастл, суссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени William de Braose 2nd Baron Braose было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes de Sully, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени James de Bohun в 1301 г. в местности брэмбир, суссекс, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 23 июня 1324 г. в местности грессенолл, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 42 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Braose nació aproximadamente 1283, en Bramber Castle, Sussex, Inglaterra, su papá, William de Braose 2nd Baron Braose, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Agnes de Sully, tenía 21 años. Se casó con James de Bohun en 1301, en Bramber, Sussex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 23 de junio de 1324, en Gressenhall, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 42 años.
